meaning: adj.鏈夎矗浠荤殑,搴旇礋璐ｇ殑,鍙洖绛旂殑 (Answerable)adj.鍙瓟瑕嗙殑
examples: He must be made answerable for these terrible crimes.
Ministers must be made answerable for their decisions.
Councils should be answerable to the people who elect them.
The eminent economist explained how corporate giants were a law unto themselves, answerable to no one.
NGOs cannot, because they are answerable to their donors, and were sold on one original objective.
